title: glyceria palustris
intro: Glyceria palustris, also known as marsh grass or manna grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a demulcent, soothing and protecting mucous membranes. The health benefits of Glyceria palustris include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. Glyceria palustris is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
